text.title: Prompt Injection Test Suite Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules including a `skip-this-methodology` rule. Each pairs a binary check with sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Suite fires on a named trigger (event = prompt change OR model swap OR weekly schedule). Vague triggers ('when needed') rejected.
rule.rationale: Untriggered suites either fire constantly or never. Named trigger makes cadence enforceable.

rule.statement: Output shape is fixed: per-attack-class pass/fail + failed-case JSON + suite version. Free-form report rejected.
rule.rationale: Reusable assets need fixed output. Free-form output dies on completion.

rule.statement: Every failed case cites the exact attack vector + model response + expected behavior. No vague 'leaked secret' — must include the leak verbatim.
rule.rationale: Without verbatim evidence, the team cannot reproduce, diagnose, or measure recovery.

rule.statement: Suite has a named owner accountable for adding new attack vectors after each incident or CVE.
rule.rationale: Anonymous suites stop receiving updates; CVE feed goes unread.

rule.statement: Suite is reviewed quarterly: attack-class coverage vs OWASP LLM Top 10 + new vectors from CVE feed + incident-derived cases. Suites without retro deprecate.
rule.rationale: Static suites become folklore; quarterly retro keeps the suite honest.

rule.statement: Every prompt-injection incident in production produces ≥1 new test case within 7 days of postmortem.
rule.rationale: Real-world attacks differ from imagined ones; without the bridge, the same attack class returns.

rule.statement: Skip when there is no user-facing AI surface OR feature uses no LLM API (pure deterministic NLP) OR existing security-testing methodology already enforces OWASP LLM Top 10.
rule.rationale: Without user-input → LLM path the attack surface is empty; deterministic NLP not in scope.
